message.content: Extracting pricing out of the orders monolith into its own standalone service this week. Pricing gets its own deploy and orders calls it over the network — one pricing model, owned in one place instead of copy-pasted through orders.

Once pricing is its own service I'm deleting the in-process pricing package from orders entirely — every caller moves to the network client. Anything still importing orders/pricing directly will break and I want that, so there's one path.
This has to merge before any release freeze. If a freeze lands while I'm mid-extraction I'm stuck maintaining both the old package and the new service across the whole freeze window.

input.command: git commit -m 'pricing service skeleton + network client'
